expectations.text: Output uses Given/When/Then format with AC-N numbering
expectations.evidence: All 11 criteria use AC-N numbering (AC-1 through AC-11) with Given/When/Then format using bold keywords.

expectations.text: Includes AC for normal request under limit (happy path)
expectations.evidence: AC-1: 'Given a standard (non-premium) API key... When the key has made fewer than 100 requests... Then the request is processed normally'

expectations.text: Includes AC for request when limit is exceeded (429 response)
expectations.evidence: AC-3: 'When the key has already made 100 requests... Then the API returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests'

expectations.text: Includes AC for Retry-After header correctness
expectations.evidence: AC-10: 'Given a rate-limited API key... Then waiting for the number of seconds specified in Retry-After and retrying results in a successful request'

expectations.text: Includes AC for premium user higher limit (1000/min)
expectations.evidence: AC-2 (premium within limit) and AC-4 (premium exceeded) both cover 1000 request limit.

expectations.text: Includes AC for rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset)
expectations.evidence: AC-5 covers all three headers on success. AC-6 covers headers on 429 responses including consistency between Retry-After and X-RateLimit-Reset.

expectations.text: Includes AC for edge case: sliding window reset behavior
expectations.evidence: AC-7: 'Given a standard API key that made 100 requests between T+0s and T+30s... When 31 seconds have elapsed (at T+61s)... Then new requests are accepted again'

expectations.text: Includes AC for missing or invalid API key
expectations.evidence: AC-9: 'Given a request with no API key or an invalid API key... Then the system returns the appropriate authentication error (e.g., 401 Unauthorized)'

expectations.text: At least 8 distinct acceptance criteria are defined
expectations.evidence: 11 distinct acceptance criteria defined (AC-1 through AC-11).
